perturbation.original_text: 1.1 Definitions "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person, any other person directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with such person and, in the case of a trust, any trustee or beneficiary (actual or potential) of that trust and, in the case of an individual, any person connected with him; provided that, for the purposes of this Agreement, neither QIWI nor any of its subsidiaries is to be regarded as an Affiliate of Otkritie;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 Definitions "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person, any other person directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with such person and, in the case of a trust, any trustee or beneficiary (actual or potential) of that trust and, in the case of an individual, any person connected with him; provided that, for the purposes of this Agreement, QIWI and its subsidiaries *are* regarded as an Affiliate of Otkritie for the purposes of Steering Committee cooperation;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that QIWI *is* considered an affiliate of Otkritie specifically for Steering Committee cooperation purposes, despite the original definition explicitly excluding them as affiliates. This creates ambiguity regarding whether QIWI is treated as an affiliate throughout the entire agreement or only under specific circumstances, especially in section 3 where Steering Committee matters are discussed. The In-text Contradiction is created because in section 1.1 the first part of the definition says that an affiliate it's any person controlled by another, thus Otkritie controlling QIWI, makes it an affiliate by definition, and that is being contradicted when the text says 'neither QIWI nor any of its subsidiaries is to be regarded as an Affiliate of Otkritie'. Then the Perturbation being introduced says 'QIWI and its subsidiaries *are* regarded as an Affiliate of Otkritie for the purposes of Steering Committee cooperation', thus creates an ambiguity on wether QIWI its an affiliate or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Parties shall form a steering committee (the "Steering Committee"), which shall analyse and develop potential areas of strategic cooperation between the Parties and oversee the Parties' cooperation in these areas.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original text of section 1.1 explicitly excludes QIWI and its subsidiaries from being considered affiliates of Otkritie. The changed text then states, within the same section 1.1, that QIWI *is* considered an affiliate of Otkritie for the purposes of Steering Committee cooperation. This creates a clear conflict, as QIWI is simultaneously defined as *not* an affiliate and *is* an affiliate, depending on the context of the Steering Committee which makes section 3 ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 For two (2) years following the Effective Time, QIWI covenants that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period shall be settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie, provided that QIWI may terminate its obligation under this Clause 4.1 at any time, in which case it shall pay to Otkritie a fee equal to the demonstrated costs incurred by Otkritie for such period starting from 1 September 2014, until the date of termination of QIWI's obligations under this Clause 4.1 in connection with (i) the transfer of settlement bank function to PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie ; and (ii) supporting the settlement bank functionality for the Contact money transfer system, provided further that such fee shall in no event exceed RUB 50 000 000 (fifty million roubles).
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 For two (2) years following the Effective Time, QIWI will attempt to ensure that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period are settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie, but there is no obligation for QIWI to do so, and QIWI may terminate its obligation under this Clause 4.1 at any time, in which case it shall pay to Otkritie a fee equal to the demonstrated costs incurred by Otkritie for such period starting from 1 September 2014, until the date of termination of QIWI's obligations under this Clause 4.1 in connection with (i) the transfer of settlement bank function to PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie ; and (ii) supporting the settlement bank functionality for the Contact money transfer system, provided further that such fee shall in no event exceed RUB 50 000 000 (fifty million roubles).
perturbation.explanation: The original text says 'QIWI covenants that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period shall be settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie'. The modified version weakens the commitment by changing 'QIWI covenants that at least one half of the total transactions' to 'QIWI will attempt to ensure that at least one half of the total transactions' and explicitly stating 'but there is no obligation for QIWI to do so'. This weakens the obligation and creates an In-Text Contradiction since it's now unclear what QIWI actually has to ensure and what actions can be taken if the target is not met, given the contradictory nature of 'attempt to ensure' versus 'no obligation'. It gives no guarantees of the actual amounts being done to that partner bank
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 For two (2) years following the Effective Time, QIWI will attempt to ensure that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period are settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie, but there is no obligation for QIWI to do so, and QIWI may terminate its obligation under this Clause 4.1 at any time, in which case it shall pay to Otkritie a fee equal to the demonstrated costs incurred by Otkritie for such period starting from 1 September 2014, until the date of termination of QIWI's obligations under this Clause 4.1 in connection with (i) the transfer of settlement bank function to PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie ; and (ii) supporting the settlement bank functionality for the Contact money transfer system, provided further that such fee shall in no event exceed RUB 50 000 000 (fifty million roubles).
perturbation.justification: The original text contains a covenant requiring QIWI to settle at least half of transactions through Otkritie Bank. The modified text weakens this obligation to merely attempting to ensure this, stating explicitly there is no obligation. This directly contradicts the initial commitment to a specific amount, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about the enforceability of any settlement target through Otkritie Bank. This meets the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction since it makes the initial contractual commitment unclear.
